title: Whitened-motif inconsistency across sibling pages
oneline: Motif strips confirmed outliers; notes-driven re-chalk matched sibling treatment first try
summary: Built an offline whitened-region inventory (per-page bboxes of everything each chalk whitened, all 94 pages, no API) plus a motif contact-strip tool that renders side-by-side chalk-display and night-composite crops from a small registry. It confirmed rectangle-wide's eyeball-donut bubbles and surfaced new inconsistencies (planets, stars, flowers; the pterodactyl suns genuinely differ). A single --notes re-chalk of shapes/rectangle-wide converged to the sibling's thin-ring treatment on the first attempt (keep 100%, white 0%), and a night-fill regen against the new chalk completed the loop — 4 of 8 Gemini calls used.
tried: Offline whitened-region inventory over all 94 chalked pages (reusing the chalk gate's ink/flood definitions)
Motif contact strips: chalk display + night composite crops side by side for bubbles/sun/stars/planets/flowers
Sibling tall-vs-wide whitened-area ratio as an outlier triage heuristic
Re-chalk of shapes/rectangle-wide with --notes naming the motif and the sibling's treatment (t 0.25)
Night-fill regen against the candidate chalk (shipped chalk temporarily swapped, then git-restored)
worked: Inventory + strips make the inconsistencies obvious in minutes, fully offline
Notes-driven re-chalk converged to the sibling treatment in ONE attempt, all gates green
End-to-end: regenerated night fill passed gates in 3 tries; final composite matches sibling's glowing thin-ring bubbles
Found new unlisted inconsistencies: space planets (solid vs outlined), stars (moon-wide solid vs others outlined), flowers (heart-tall solid petals)
failed: No fully-automatic motif grouping: naming motifs and locating non-whitened sibling occurrences needs a human (bboxes hand-picked for those)
IDEAS.md's example was partly wrong: square-wide has no bubbles (clouds); pterodactyl-wide has no rayed sun (its sun is an outlined ring filled gold)
limitations: Old night fill under a new chalk leaks its own whites through the punch, so every chalk fix requires a night-fill regen (1-5 extra Gemini calls)
Sibling-asymmetry ratio flags legitimate composition differences too (spider web) - triage, not a gate
Which treatment is canon (solid glowing white vs outlined+fill glow) is an art call the tooling cannot make
Nothing shipped: candidates live in gitignored .coloring-samples-dark/ for a follow-up session
